guidance.agency_name: Pennsylvania Department of Health / Pennsylvania Department of Environmental Protection
guidance.repeat_testing_guidance: Pennsylvania does not publish a single universal PFAS retesting cadence for all private wells in Sharp v1. Keep repeat testing tied to accredited-lab interpretation, Pennsylvania agency guidance, and any site-specific investigation follow-up.
guidance.benchmark_notes: Pennsylvania private wells are owner-managed, not regulated public-water systems. Pennsylvania's PFAS MCL rule can supply reference context for covered analytes, but private-well outputs should stay reference-based and should not be framed as compliance findings.

profile.primary_reference_label: Pennsylvania PFAS MCL rule used as private-well reference context
profile.summary: Pennsylvania adopted PFAS MCLs for PFOA and PFOS in public water, while private wells remain owner-managed. For Sharp v1, Pennsylvania can provide direct numeric reference context for covered analytes, but the route should still keep current state and federal transition notes visible and avoid presenting a private-well compliance determination.

benchmarks.label: Pennsylvania MCL for PFOA
benchmarks.note: Pennsylvania's PFAS MCL rule set PFOA at 14 ppt for public-water compliance context.

benchmarks.label: Pennsylvania MCL for PFOS
benchmarks.note: Pennsylvania's PFAS MCL rule set PFOS at 18 ppt for public-water compliance context.

profile.private_well_use_note: Use Pennsylvania's PFOA and PFOS MCL values as reference context only. Private wells are not public-water systems, so the household path should stay anchored in accredited-lab interpretation, state guidance, and PFAS-specific follow-up rather than in a compliance-style verdict.

entry_decision_input.summary: No direct well test is available yet, so the engine should route the household to a state-guided lab and sampling workflow before any product comparison.
